action.reasoning: Observation: Persistent token/feature mismatch error (even when debug counts match) after 17 iters; both train.py and predict.py are truncated in workspace (incomplete loop/optimizer/save/metrics/logging after debug print in train.py; incomplete point coord handling + no JSON dump at end in predict.py). test_prep confirms ~7k samples with bbox/point targets in abs pixels. Analysis: Truncation means the executed code after the debug print is incomplete/syntax-broken so inputs to model.forward are malformed (bad shapes for pixel_values/image_grid_thw/labels or missing unsqueeze/batch handling), causing internal placeholder mask failure in Qwen2VL even if counts appear to match in partial print. Dual processor calls can also cause subtle template diffs. Root fix: provide FULL complete non-truncated scripts. Use single full-conversation processor call (guarantees token/feature alignment), robust assistant header token search for prompt_length (no second processor call), explicit tensor batching/unsqueeze, correct dtype moves, smaller fixed pixels=128*28*28, bs=1 manual loop (64 samples/32 steps ~20s), AdamW, metrics.jsonl logging per guidelines, full adapter+processor+grid save. Complete predict.py with all fallback parsers, full point/bbox rescale from 1000-grid using per-row w/h, clipping, center default, and exact JSON output per eval contract. This follows standard Qwen2VL SFT for grounding and will produce first successful baseline + grounding_score. Decision: Overwrite train.py and predict.py with complete working code, run test_prep+train, submit_eval for first score and time measurement before scaling data/epochs/LR/rank.
